title: 'Final Project'
author: 'Cassie & Hani'
format: html
---

0. Loading
```{python}
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
import os
import geopandas as gpd
from shapely.geometry import Point
import numpy as np

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

import rasterio
from rasterio.warp import calculate_default_transform, reproject, Resampling
from rasterio.mask import mask
from rasterstats import zonal_stats
```

# For SocioEconomic Vulnerability Index
1. Reading in all the file
```{python}
DATA_CLEAN_DIR = Path("data/derived-data")
DATA_DIR = Path("data/raw-data")

income = pd.read_csv(DATA_CLEAN_DIR / "income_clean.csv")
renter = pd.read_csv(DATA_CLEAN_DIR / "renter_clean.csv")
housing_age = pd.read_csv(DATA_CLEAN_DIR / "housing_age_clean.csv")
home_value = pd.read_csv(DATA_CLEAN_DIR / "home_value_clean.csv")

tracts = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / 'tl_2023_17_tract'/ "tl_2023_17_tract.shp")

```

2. Clip All Dataframe into Chicago Shape
```{python}
tracts = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / 'tl_2023_17_tract'/ "tl_2023_17_tract.shp")
tracts["GEOID"] = tracts["GEOID"].astype(str)
cook_tracts = tracts[tracts["COUNTYFP"] == "031"].copy()

def make_it_geo(df, base_map):
    df["GEOID"] = df["GEOID"].astype(str)
    return base_map.merge(df, on="GEOID", how="inner")

gdf_income = make_it_geo(income, cook_tracts)
gdf_renter = make_it_geo(renter, cook_tracts)
gdf_age = make_it_geo(housing_age, cook_tracts)
gdf_home = make_it_geo(home_value, cook_tracts)

city = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / "Boundaries_Chicago.geojson")
city = city.to_crs(gdf_income.crs)
city_union = city.geometry.unary_union

def clip_to_chicago_and_save(gdf, name):
    gdf = gdf.to_crs(city.crs)
    
    gdf_chi = gdf[gdf.geometry.centroid.within(city_union)].copy()
    
    # as csv
    cols_to_save = [c for c in gdf_chi.columns if c != 'geometry']
    csv_filename = f"{name}_chicago.csv"
    gdf_chi[cols_to_save].to_csv(csv_filename, index=False)
    
    # as gpkg
    gdf_chi.crs = "EPSG:4326" 
    gpkg_filename = f"{name}_chicago.gpkg"
    gdf_chi.to_file(gpkg_filename, layer='tracts', driver="GPKG")
    
    print(f" {name} done:")
    print(f"   - Number of Tracts being kept: {len(gdf_chi)}")
    print(f"   - Saved: {csv_filename} & {gpkg_filename}")
    return gdf_chi

gdfs = {"income_24c": gdf_income, "renter_24c": gdf_renter, "house_age_24c": gdf_age, "home_value_24c": gdf_home}
chicago_gdfs = {} 

for name, target_gdf in gdfs.items():
    chicago_gdfs[name] = clip_to_chicago_and_save(target_gdf, name)

income_chi = chicago_gdfs["income_24c"]
renter_chi = chicago_gdfs["renter_24c"]
age_chi = chicago_gdfs["house_age_24c"]
home_value_chi = chicago_gdfs["home_value_24c"]

```

3. Standardizing all variables
```{python}
def zscore(series):
    return (series - series.mean()) / series.std()

income_chi['income_z'] = -zscore(income_chi['median_income'])
renter_chi['renter_z'] = zscore(renter_chi['renter_share'])
age_chi['age_z'] = zscore(age_chi['old_housing_share'])
home_value_chi['home_value_z'] = -zscore(home_value_chi['median_home_value'])

```

4. Sanity Check for each variable
```{python}
print(income_chi['income_z'].describe())
print(renter_chi['renter_z'].describe())
print(age_chi['age_z'].describe())
print(home_value_chi['home_value_z'].describe())
```

5. Setting up the Socio Index
```{python}
socio = income_chi[['GEOID', 'geometry', 'income_z']].merge(
    renter_chi[['GEOID', 'renter_z']],
    on='GEOID', how = 'inner').merge(
    age_chi[['GEOID', 'age_z']],
    on='GEOID', how = 'inner').merge(
    home_value_chi[['GEOID', 'home_value_z']],
    on='GEOID', how = 'inner')

socio['socio_index'] = (
    2 * socio['income_z'] +
    0.5 * socio['renter_z'] +
    0.5 * socio['age_z'] +
    2 * socio['home_value_z']) / 5

socio['socio_index'].describe()
```

6. Mapping socio vulnerability index
```{python}
socio["socio_percentile"] = (
    socio["socio_index"]
    .rank(pct=True)
)

max_abs = abs(socio['socio_index']).max()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7,7))

socio.plot(
    column='socio_percentile',
    cmap='YlOrRd',
    linewidth = 0.1,
    legend=True,
    legend_kwds={"label": "Socioeconomic Vulnerability Percentile\n(0 = lowest exposure, 1 = highest exposure)","orientation": "vertical"},
    ax=ax
)

ax.set_title("Socioeconomic Vulnerability Index Percentile by Census Tract\nChicago, IL",
    fontsize=14,
    pad=12)

ax.set_axis_off()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# For Environmental Exposure Index

1. Pre-processing (CRS + Clipping)
ENV_RAW_DIR = Path("data/raw-data")
ENV_DERIVED_DIR = Path("data/derived-data")
ENV_DERIVED_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

TARGET_CRS = "EPSG:3435"

TRACTS_PATH  = ENV_RAW_DIR / "tl_2022_17_tract.shp"
CHICAGO_PATH = ENV_RAW_DIR / "Boundaries_Chicago.geojson"
ROADS_PATH   = ENV_RAW_DIR / "tl_2022_17_prisecroads.shp"
TRI_CSV_PATH = ENV_RAW_DIR / "EPA_TRI_IL_2022.csv"

RASTERS = {
    "lst_summer":  ENV_RAW_DIR / "chicago_summer_LST.tif",
    "lst_winter":  ENV_RAW_DIR / "chicago_winter_LST.tif",
    "ndvi_summer": ENV_RAW_DIR / "chicago_summer_NDVI.tif",
    "impervious":  ENV_RAW_DIR / "chicago_impervious.tif",
}

OUT_TRACTS_GPKG = ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__tracts_chicago_2022.gpkg"
OUT_ROADS_GPKG  = ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__roads_chicago_2022.gpkg"
OUT_TRI_GPKG    = ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__tri_chicago_2022.gpkg"

def _drop_empty(gdf: gpd.GeoDataFrame) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:
    return gdf[gdf.geometry.notna() & ~gdf.geometry.is_empty].copy()

def _fix_geom(gdf: gpd.GeoDataFrame) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:
    gdf = gdf.copy()
    gdf["geometry"] = gdf.geometry.make_valid()
    return gdf

def _dissolve_to_single_polygon(gdf: gpd.GeoDataFrame) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:
    return gdf.dissolve()

def _reproject_and_clip_raster_to_mask(
    src_path: Path,
    dst_path: Path,
    mask_gdf: gpd.GeoDataFrame,
    target_crs: str = TARGET_CRS
) -> None:
    """
    Reproject raster to target CRS, then clip to mask geometry.
    Writes GeoTIFF to dst_path.
    """
    with rasterio.open(src_path) as src:
        if src.crs is None:
            raise ValueError(f"Raster has no CRS: {src_path}")

        transform, width, height = calculate_default_transform(
            src.crs, target_crs, src.width, src.height, *src.bounds
        )

        dst_meta = src.meta.copy()
        dst_meta.update({
            "crs": target_crs,
            "transform": transform,
            "width": width,
            "height": height
        })

        tmp_path = ENV_DERIVED_DIR / f"__tmp_reproject_{src_path.stem}.tif"
        with rasterio.open(tmp_path, "w", **dst_meta) as dst:
            for i in range(1, src.count + 1):
                reproject(
                    source=rasterio.band(src, i),
                    destination=rasterio.band(dst, i),
                    src_transform=src.transform,
                    src_crs=src.crs,
                    dst_transform=transform,
                    dst_crs=target_crs,
                    resampling=Resampling.bilinear,
                )

    with rasterio.open(tmp_path) as reproj:
        geoms = list(mask_gdf.geometry)
        out_img, out_transform = mask(reproj, geoms, crop=True)
        out_meta = reproj.meta.copy()
        out_meta.update({
            "height": out_img.shape[1],
            "width": out_img.shape[2],
            "transform": out_transform
        })

    with rasterio.open(dst_path, "w", **out_meta) as out:
        out.write(out_img)

    try:
        tmp_path.unlink()
    except OSError:
        pass

def _add_zonal_mean(gdf: gpd.GeoDataFrame, raster_path: Path, out_col: str) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:
    zs = zonal_stats(gdf, raster_path, stats=["mean"], all_touched=False)
    gdf[out_col] = [d["mean"] for d in zs]
    return gdf


env_tracts = _fix_geom(_drop_empty(gpd.read_file(TRACTS_PATH)))
env_chicago = _fix_geom(_drop_empty(gpd.read_file(CHICAGO_PATH)))

env_tracts = env_tracts.to_crs(TARGET_CRS)
env_chicago = env_chicago.to_crs(TARGET_CRS)

env_chicago_poly = _dissolve_to_single_polygon(env_chicago)
env_tracts_chicago = gpd.clip(env_tracts, env_chicago_poly)
print("Chicago tracts:", len(env_tracts_chicago))

env_roads = _fix_geom(_drop_empty(gpd.read_file(ROADS_PATH))).to_crs(TARGET_CRS)
env_roads_chicago = gpd.clip(env_roads, env_chicago_poly)
print("Road segments (clipped):", len(env_roads_chicago))

env_tri = pd.read_csv(TRI_CSV_PATH)
env_tri = env_tri.dropna(subset=["12. LATITUDE", "13. LONGITUDE"]).copy()

env_tri_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    env_tri,
    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(env_tri["13. LONGITUDE"], env_tri["12. LATITUDE"]),
    crs="EPSG:4326"
).to_crs(TARGET_CRS)

env_tri_gdf = gpd.clip(env_tri_gdf, env_chicago_poly)
print("TRI points (clipped):", len(env_tri_gdf))

env_tracts_chicago.to_file(OUT_TRACTS_GPKG, layer="tracts", driver="GPKG")
env_roads_chicago.to_file(OUT_ROADS_GPKG, layer="roads", driver="GPKG")
env_tri_gdf.to_file(OUT_TRI_GPKG, layer="tri", driver="GPKG")


for name, path in RASTERS.items():
    out_path = ENV_DERIVED_DIR / f"env__{name}_3435_clip.tif"
    _reproject_and_clip_raster_to_mask(path, out_path, env_chicago_poly, target_crs=TARGET_CRS)

print("Rasters reprojected & clipped to", TARGET_CRS)


env_tracts = gpd.read_file(OUT_TRACTS_GPKG, layer="tracts")
env_roads  = gpd.read_file(OUT_ROADS_GPKG, layer="roads")
env_tri    = gpd.read_file(OUT_TRI_GPKG, layer="tri")

env_tracts = _add_zonal_mean(env_tracts, ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__lst_summer_3435_clip.tif", "lst_summer_mean")
env_tracts = _add_zonal_mean(env_tracts, ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__lst_winter_3435_clip.tif", "lst_winter_mean")
env_tracts = _add_zonal_mean(env_tracts, ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__ndvi_summer_3435_clip.tif", "ndvi_mean")
env_tracts = _add_zonal_mean(env_tracts, ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__impervious_3435_clip.tif", "impervious_mean")

env_roads_x = gpd.overlay(
    env_roads[["geometry"]],
    env_tracts[["GEOID", "geometry"]],
    how="intersection"
)
env_roads_x["road_len_ft"] = env_roads_x.geometry.length

env_road_sum = env_roads_x.groupby("GEOID")["road_len_ft"].sum().reset_index()

env_tracts["tract_area_ft2"] = env_tracts.geometry.area
env_tracts = env_tracts.merge(env_road_sum, on="GEOID", how="left")
env_tracts["road_len_ft"] = env_tracts["road_len_ft"].fillna(0)
env_tracts["road_density"] = env_tracts["road_len_ft"] / env_tracts["tract_area_ft2"]

env_tri = env_tri.copy()
env_tri["total_releases"] = pd.to_numeric(env_tri["107. TOTAL RELEASES"], errors="coerce").fillna(0)

env_tri_join = gpd.sjoin(
    env_tri,
    env_tracts[["GEOID", "geometry"]],
    how="inner",
    predicate="within"
)

env_tri_summary = (
    env_tri_join.groupby("GEOID")
    .agg(
        tri_release_total=("total_releases", "sum"),
        tri_facility_count=("2. TRIFD", "nunique")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

env_tracts = env_tracts.merge(env_tri_summary, on="GEOID", how="left")
env_tracts["tri_release_total"] = env_tracts["tri_release_total"].fillna(0)
env_tracts["tri_facility_count"] = env_tracts["tri_facility_count"].fillna(0)

env_tracts.to_file(ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__metrics_tracts.gpkg", layer="tracts", driver="GPKG")
env_tracts.drop(columns="geometry").to_csv(ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__metrics_tracts.csv", index=False)

print("\n✅ Done. Environmental metrics saved to:")
print(" -", (ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__metrics_tracts.gpkg"))
print(" -", (ENV_DERIVED_DIR / "env__metrics_tracts.csv"))

2. Load tract-level environmental metrics

```{python}
DERIVED_DIR = Path("data/derived-data")
RAW_DIR = Path("data/raw-data")

env = gpd.read_file(DERIVED_DIR / "env__metrics_tracts.gpkg", layer="tracts")

expected_cols = [
    "GEOID",
    "lst_summer_mean",
    "lst_winter_mean",
    "ndvi_mean",
    "impervious_mean",
    "road_density",
    "tri_release_total",
    "tri_facility_count",
]
missing = [c for c in expected_cols if c not in env.columns]
print("Missing columns:", missing)

print("\nRows:", len(env))
print("\nBasic summary (selected columns):")
print(env[["lst_summer_mean","ndvi_mean","impervious_mean","road_density","tri_release_total"]].describe())
```

3. Cleaning + transformations

```{python}
env = env.dropna(subset=["lst_summer_mean", "ndvi_mean", "impervious_mean"]).copy()
env["ndvi_bad"] = -env["ndvi_mean"]
env["tri_release_log"] = np.log1p(env["tri_release_total"])

print("\nAfter cleaning, rows:", len(env))
```

4. Z-score standardization + weighted exposure index

```{python}
vars_for_index = [
    "lst_summer_mean",
    "ndvi_bad",
    "impervious_mean",
    "road_density",
    "tri_release_log",
]

for v in vars_for_index:
    env[f"z_{v}"] = (env[v] - env[v].mean()) / env[v].std()

weights = {
    "z_lst_summer_mean": 0.25,
    "z_ndvi_bad": 0.125,
    "z_impervious_mean": 0.125,
    "z_road_density": 0.25,
    "z_tri_release_log": 0.25,
}

env["env_exposure_index"] = sum(env[k] * w for k, w in weights.items())

env["exposure_percentile"] = env["env_exposure_index"].rank(pct=True)

print("\nExposure index summary:")
print(env["env_exposure_index"].describe())
```

5. Visualization

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7,7))

env.plot(
    column="exposure_percentile",
    cmap="viridis",
    legend=True,
    linewidth=0.05,
    edgecolor="black",
    ax=ax,
    legend_kwds={
        "label": "Environmental Exposure Percentile\n(0 = lowest exposure, 1 = highest exposure)",
        "orientation": "vertical"
    }
)

ax.set_title("Environmental Exposure Index Percentile by Census Tract\nChicago")
ax.set_axis_off()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

6. Join to community areas

```{python}
community_path = RAW_DIR / "Boundaries_-_Community_Areas_20260228.geojson"

if community_path.exists():
    community = gpd.read_file(community_path).to_crs(env.crs)

    for c in ["index_right"]:
        if c in env.columns:
            env = env.drop(columns=c)

    env_ca = gpd.sjoin(env, community[["community", "geometry"]], how="left", predicate="within")

    missing_ca = env_ca["community"].isna().sum()
    print("\nTracts not assigned to community (within):", missing_ca)

    if missing_ca > 0:
        unmatched = env_ca[env_ca["community"].isna()].drop(columns=["community"]).copy()
        matched = env_ca[~env_ca["community"].isna()].copy()

        unmatched = gpd.sjoin(unmatched.drop(columns=["index_right"], errors="ignore"),
                              community[["community", "geometry"]],
                              how="left",
                              predicate="intersects")

        unmatched = unmatched.sort_values("GEOID").drop_duplicates(subset=["GEOID"])

        env_ca = pd.concat([matched, unmatched], ignore_index=True)

    community_summary = (
        env_ca.dropna(subset=["community"])
              .groupby("community")
              .agg(
                  mean_exposure=("env_exposure_index", "mean"),
                  p90_exposure=("env_exposure_index", lambda x: np.quantile(x, 0.9)),
                  n_tracts=("GEOID", "nunique"),
              )
              .sort_values("mean_exposure", ascending=False)
              .reset_index()
    )

    print("\nTop 10 community areas by mean exposure (tract-average):")
    print(community_summary.head(10))
else:
    print("\nCommunity areas file not found. Skipping community summary.")
```

7. Final outputs

```{python}
env.to_file(DERIVED_DIR / "env__exposure_final.gpkg", layer="tracts", driver="GPKG")
env.drop(columns="geometry").to_csv(DERIVED_DIR / "env__exposure_final.csv", index=False)

print("\n✅ Saved final outputs:")
print(" -", DERIVED_DIR / "env__exposure_final.gpkg")
print(" -", DERIVED_DIR / "env__exposure_final.csv")
```


# Combine 2 index & Visualization

1. Combining two indexes
```{python}
socio.columns
tracts.columns

socio['GEOID'] = socio['GEOID'].astype(str)
tracts['GEOID'] = tracts['GEOID'].astype(str)

combined = tracts.merge(
    socio[['GEOID','socio_index']], 
    on='GEOID', 
    how='inner'
)
combined.columns
combined.head()
```

2. Add in community name in combined files
```{python}
community = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / "Boundaries_-_Community_Areas_20260228.geojson")

# match CRS with tracts (important)
community = community.to_crs(combined.crs)

# remove any leftover index_right column (prevents join error)
if "index_right" in combined.columns:
    combined = combined.drop(columns="index_right")

# spatial join
combined = gpd.sjoin(combined,
    community[["community", "geometry"]],
    how="left",
    predicate="intersects"
)

# quick check
print(combined.head())
combined.columns

combined.to_file("DATA_CLEAN_DIR / combined.geojson", driver="GeoJSON")
```

3. Visualization for double indexes scatter plot
```{python}
#quadrant + label + palette
combined["quadrant"] = (
    (combined["socio_index"] > 0).astype(int) * 2 +
    (combined["env_exposure_index"] > 0).astype(int)
)

quadrant_labels = {
    0: "Low Socio – Low Exposure(LL)",
    1: "Low Socio – High Exposure(LH)",
    2: "High Socio – Low Exposure(HL)",
    3: "High Socio – High Exposure(HH)"
}
combined["quadrant_label"] = combined["quadrant"].map(quadrant_labels)

palette = {
    "Low Socio – Low Exposure(LL)": "#d9d9d9",
    "Low Socio – High Exposure(LH)": "#80b1d3",
    "High Socio – Low Exposure(HL)": "#fdb462",
    "High Socio – High Exposure(HH)": "#e41a1c"
}

#Altair scatter + regression + reference lines
base = alt.Chart(combined).properties(
    width=520,   
    height=520,
    title="Environmental Exposure vs Socioeconomic Vulnerability"
)

points = base.mark_circle(opacity=0.7, size=30).encode(
    x=alt.X("socio_index:Q", title="Socioeconomic Vulnerability"),
    y=alt.Y("env_exposure_index:Q", title="Environmental Exposure"),
    color=alt.Color(
        "quadrant_label:N",
        title="Typology",
        scale=alt.Scale(domain=list(palette.keys()), range=list(palette.values())),
        legend=alt.Legend(
            orient='none',      
            legendX=10,          
            legendY=400,         
            fillColor='white',   
            padding=10,          
            strokeColor='gray')  
    ),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("quadrant_label:N", title="Typology"),
        alt.Tooltip("socio_index:Q", title="Socio", format=".3f"),
        alt.Tooltip("env_exposure_index:Q", title="Exposure", format=".3f"),
        alt.Tooltip("community:N", title="Community")  
    ]
)

# regression line 
reg_line = base.transform_regression(
    "socio_index", "env_exposure_index"
).mark_line(color="black").encode(
    x="socio_index:Q",
    y="env_exposure_index:Q"
)

# reference lines at x=0 and y=0
vline = alt.Chart(pd.DataFrame({"x": [0]})).mark_rule(color="black").encode(x="x:Q")
hline = alt.Chart(pd.DataFrame({"y": [0]})).mark_rule(color="black").encode(y="y:Q")

chart = (points + reg_line + vline + hline)

chart
```
